content.claim: Central admission statistics show 4th-grade (8-year track) applicants fell >14% year-on-year, while 6th-grade (6-year track) applications rose 11%; total gimnázium enrolment (grades 5–8) moved only ~300 students over four years (25,800 to 26,100), and 2026 general secondary admissions involved 89,541 students, supporting a demographic argument that the selection exam's original rationing function is eroding.
content.context: Selection reform is now live government policy with EU tailwind but faces low-probability cross-party durability, high reversal risk on government turnover, and non-negotiable stakeholder preconditions (workforce guarantees, statutory funding) that the minister has not yet addressed, making it politically feasible in the medium term (2027–2028) only if the government narrows its scope to pilot-phase rebalancing coupled with explicit quality-levelling commitments, abandons abolition rhetoric, and negotiates binding funding guarantees—the opposite of which suggests either stalled-indefinite consultation or legislative reversal within one electoral cycle.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
